architecture_fit.note: Snap-to-group (template argmax + modular addition) directly tests the group-law hypothesis and solves it; minimal zero-block circuit, though it discretizes rather than exercising softmax attention itself.

baseline_comparison.note: Naive A@B baseline computed under identical conditions; clear lift at canonical (0.988 vs 0.658, lift 0.33) shown as two curves.

faithfulness.note: Purely synthetic, no model; README explicitly says so and proposes a concrete ablation (drop group composition), but runs no actual causal check.

operating_range.note: Full noise sweep σ=0→40 (clean→chance) shows graceful degradation (1.0→0.74) and where it breaks; only n=6 group size tested.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Fully hand-built: fixed rotation templates plus modular-addition rule, no learned weights — exactly the hardcoded-circuit case.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab leads with the method-vs-baseline fidelity curve (canonical marked) plus A/B/pred/true heatmaps — legible and on-point.

visualisation_rationale.note: README ties every axis to the benchmark: fidelity y-axis, full-sweep x-axis, vertical gap = lift metric, heatmap as concrete permutation check.

notes: A clean hand-built snap-to-group composer that directly operationalizes the group-law hypothesis and decisively beats the naive-matmul baseline at canonical noise (lift 0.33), with graceful degradation across the full noise sweep. The hardcoded circuit and tightly-justified visualisation are strengths; the main gap is faithfulness — no causal/ablation evidence, only a proposed check, which is acceptable for a synthetic attempt. Mean of scored items ≈ 4.3 places this in the 'good' band.
